title.zh: 卷三十八 張法滕馮度楊列傳
title.en: Volume 38: Biographies of Zhang, Fa, Teng, Feng, Du, Yang
title.raw: 卷三十八 張法滕馮度楊列傳 Volume 38: Biographies of Zhang, Fa, Teng, Feng, Du, Yang

sentences.zh: 張宗字諸君，南陽魯陽人也。

translations.literal: Zhang Zong, courtesy name Zhujun, was a man of Luyang in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Zong, styled Zhujun, came from Luyang in Nanyang commandery.

sentences.zh: 王莽時，為縣陽泉鄉佐。

translations.literal: In the time of Wang Mang, he served as township assistant in Yangquan township of the county.
translations.idiomatic: Under Wang Mang he held the post of township aide at Yangquan in his home county.

sentences.zh: 會莽敗，義兵起，宗乃率陽泉民三四百人起兵略地，西至長安，更始以宗為偏將軍。

translations.literal: When Mang fell and righteous armies rose, Zong then led three or four hundred men of Yangquan to raise troops and seize territory, west to Chang'an; Gengshi appointed Zong Lieutenant General.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Mang fell and loyal forces rose, Zong rallied three or four hundred men from Yangquan, took the field to seize ground, and marched west to Chang'an, where the Gengshi Emperor named him lieutenant general.

sentences.zh: 宗見更始政亂，因將家屬客安邑。

translations.literal: Zong saw Gengshi's government in chaos and therefore took his family and lodged as a guest at Anyi.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing the Gengshi regime unravel, he moved his household and settled as a guest in Anyi.

sentences.zh: 及大司徒鄧禹西征，定河東，宗詣禹自歸。

translations.literal: When Grand Minister of Education Deng Yu campaigned west and pacified Hedong, Zong went to Yu and submitted of his own accord.
translations.idiomatic: When Grand Minister of Education Deng Yu marched west and secured Hedong, Zong presented himself to Yu and placed himself under his command.

sentences.zh: 禹聞宗素多權謀，乃表為偏將軍。

translations.literal: Yu heard that Zong was habitually full of stratagems and therefore memorialized to appoint him Lieutenant General.
translations.idiomatic: Yu knew Zong for his resourcefulness and recommended him for the rank of lieutenant general.

sentences.zh: 禹軍到栒邑，赤眉大眾且至，禹以栒邑不足守，欲引師進就堅城，而眾人多畏賊追，憚為後拒。

translations.literal: Yu's army reached Xunyi; the great host of the Red Eyebrows was about to arrive. Yu thought Xunyi insufficient to defend and wished to lead the army forward to a fortified city, but many of the men feared pursuit by the bandits and shrank from serving as rear guard.
translations.idiomatic: When Yu's forces reached Xunyi and the main Red Eyebrow army closed in, Yu judged the town indefensible and wanted to withdraw to stronger walls, yet most of his men dreaded being caught from behind by the enemy and nobody wanted the rear-guard post.

sentences.zh: 禹乃書諸將名於竹簡，署其前後，亂著笥中，令各探之。

translations.literal: Yu then wrote the generals' names on bamboo slips, marked their front and back, mixed them in a chest, and ordered each to draw one.
translations.idiomatic: Yu wrote each general's name on a bamboo slip, labeled front and back, jumbled them in a box, and had the officers draw lots.

sentences.zh: 宗獨不肯探，曰：「死生有命，張宗豈辭難就逸乎！

translations.literal: Zong alone refused to draw, saying: "Life and death have their fate—would Zhang Zong decline hardship and seek ease!"
translations.idiomatic: Only Zong refused. "Fortune fixes life and death," he said; "should Zhang Zong shrink from danger and chase comfort?"

sentences.zh: 」禹歎息謂曰：「將軍有親弱在營，奈何不顧？

translations.literal: " Yu sighed and said to him: "The general has dependent kin weak in camp—how can you disregard them?"
translations.idiomatic: " Yu sighed and said, "You have vulnerable family in the camp—how can you ignore them?"

sentences.zh: 」宗曰：「愚聞一卒畢力，百人不當；

translations.literal: " Zong said: "I have heard that when one soldier exerts his full strength, a hundred men cannot withstand him;
translations.idiomatic: " Zong replied, "They say one rank-and-file soldier fighting with everything he has can hold off a hundred;

sentences.zh: 萬夫致死，可以橫行。

translations.literal: when ten thousand men fight to the death, they can march anywhere."
translations.idiomatic: ten thousand men resolved to die can sweep all before them."

sentences.zh: 宗今擁兵數千，以承大威，何遽其必敗乎！

translations.literal: Zong now commands several thousand troops under your great authority—how could defeat be certain!"
translations.idiomatic: I lead thousands under your command—why assume we are doomed?"

sentences.zh: 」遂留為後拒。

translations.literal: " He therefore remained as rear guard.
translations.idiomatic: " So he stayed behind as rear guard.

sentences.zh: 諸營既引兵，宗方勒厲軍士，堅壘壁，以死當之。

translations.literal: The camps having withdrawn their troops, Zong then exhorted the soldiers, strengthened walls and ramparts, and met the enemy with death before him.
translations.idiomatic: After the other camps marched off, Zong rallied his men, shored up the fortifications, and held the line as if his life were the stake.

sentences.zh: 禹到前縣，議曰：「以張將軍之眾，當百萬之師，猶以小雪投沸湯，雖欲戮力，其埶不全也。

translations.literal: When Yu reached the forward county, he deliberated and said: "With General Zhang's host facing a million-strong army, it is like casting little snow into boiling soup—though they wish to exert their strength together, the situation cannot hold."
translations.idiomatic: Yu reached the next county and told his staff, "Pitting Zhang's detachment against a horde in the millions is like tossing a flake of snow into a cauldron—however hard they fight, they cannot survive it."

sentences.zh: 」乃遣步騎二千人反還迎宗。

translations.literal: " He therefore sent two thousand infantry and cavalry back to fetch Zong.
translations.idiomatic: " So he dispatched two thousand foot and horse to double back and bring Zong in.

sentences.zh: 宗引兵始發，而赤眉卒至，宗與戰，卻之，乃得歸營，於是諸將服其勇。

translations.literal: Zong had just begun to lead his troops when the Red Eyebrows arrived suddenly; Zong fought them, drove them back, and was then able to return to camp—thereupon the generals admired his courage.
translations.idiomatic: Zong had barely started his march when the Red Eyebrows struck; he engaged them, threw them back, and rejoined the main camp, and the other generals stood in awe of his bravery.

sentences.zh: 及還到長安，宗夜將銳士入城襲赤眉，中矛貫胛，又轉攻諸營保，為流矢所激，皆幾至於死。

translations.literal: When they returned to Chang'an, Zong by night led picked troops into the city to strike the Red Eyebrows; he was pierced through the shoulder blade by a spear, then turned to attack the various fort camps and was struck by flying arrows—each time nearly to death.
translations.idiomatic: Back in Chang'an he led an elite night raid into the city against the Red Eyebrows; a spear ran through his shoulder blade. He went on to assault enemy stockades and was raked by stray arrows—more than once he nearly died.

sentences.zh: 及鄧禹徵還，光武以宗為京輔都尉，將突騎與征西大將軍馮異共擊關中諸營保，破之，遷河南都尉。

translations.literal: When Deng Yu was recalled, Guangwu appointed Zong Metropolitan Commandant of the capital region; he led mounted raiders with Grand General Who Conquers the West Feng Yi to jointly strike the fort camps of Guanzhong, defeated them, and was promoted to Commandant of Henan.
translations.idiomatic: After Deng Yu was withdrawn, Emperor Guangwu named Zong commandant of the capital districts; with Feng Yi, the western expedition commander, he rode against the fortified camps of Guanzhong, broke them, and was promoted to commandant of Henan.

sentences.zh: 建武六年，都尉官省，拜太中大夫。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Jianwu, the commandant office was abolished; he was appointed Grand Palace Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 6 the commandant post was eliminated, and he received appointment as grand palace grandee.

sentences.zh: 八年，潁川桑中盜賊群起，宗將兵擊定之。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, bandits rose in a crowd at Sangzhong in Yingchuan; Zong led troops to strike and pacify them.
translations.idiomatic: In year 8 bandit gangs sprang up around Sangzhong in Yingchuan; Zong took troops and suppressed them.

sentences.zh: 後青、冀盜賊屯聚山澤，宗以謁者督諸郡兵討平之。

translations.literal: Later bandits of Qing and Ji gathered in mountains and marshes; Zong, as messenger, supervised the troops of the commanderies and attacked and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when outlaws from Qingzhou and Ji province holed up in the hills and marshes, Zong, holding the office of messenger, coordinated county levies and stamped them out.

sentences.zh: 十六年，琅邪、北海盜賊復起，宗督二郡兵討之，乃設方略，明購賞，皆悉破散，於是沛、楚、東海、臨淮群賊懼其威武，相捕斬者數千人，青、徐震慄。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, bandits of Langya and Beihai rose again; Zong supervised the troops of the two commanderies to attack them, then laid plans and made clear rewards for capture—all were utterly broken and scattered. Thereupon the mass bandits of Pei, Chu, Donghai, and Linhuai feared his might; those who captured and killed one another numbered several thousand; Qing and Xu trembled.
translations.idiomatic: In year 16 unrest flared again in Langya and Beihai; Zong commanded both commanderies' forces, laid out a clear strategy and generous bounties, and scattered every band. Across Pei, Chu, Donghai, and Linhuai the gangs quailed at his reputation—rival factions turned on one another and claimed thousands of heads—and Qingzhou and Xuzhou shook with fear.

sentences.zh: 後遷琅邪相，其政好嚴猛，敢殺伐。

translations.literal: Later he was transferred to be chancellor of Langya; his administration favored severity and harshness, daring execution and killing.
translations.idiomatic: He was later promoted to chancellor of Langya, where he governed with a heavy hand and did not shrink from capital punishment.

sentences.zh: 永平二年，卒於官。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yongping, he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in the second year of the Yongping era (59 CE).

sentences.zh: 法雄字文彊，扶風郿人也，齊襄王法章之後。

translations.literal: Fa Xiong, courtesy name Wenqiang, was a man of Mei in Fufeng, a descendant of King Xiang of Qi's son Fa Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: Fa Xiong, styled Wenqiang, was a native of Mei in Fufeng and traced his line to Fa Zhang, son of King Xiang of Qi.

sentences.zh: 秦滅齊，子孫不敢稱田姓，故以法為氏。

translations.literal: When Qin destroyed Qi, the descendants dared not use the surname Tian, therefore they took Fa as the clan name.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin conquered Qi, the family dared not keep the royal surname Tian and adopted Fa instead.

sentences.zh: 宣帝時，徙三輔，世為二千石。

translations.literal: In the time of Emperor Xuan, they moved to the Three Adjuncts; for generations they held two-thousand–picul rank.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xuan they relocated to the capital region, and for generations they held top salary-grade posts.

sentences.zh: 雄初仕郡功曹，辟太傅張禹府，舉雄高第，除平氏長。

translations.literal: Xiong at first served as merit officer of the commandery, was summoned to the Grand Tutor Zhang Yu's office, was recommended in the high grade, and was appointed magistrate of Pingshi.
translations.idiomatic: Xiong began as a commandery merit officer, entered the staff of Grand Tutor Zhang Yu, graduated with high marks, and was named magistrate of Pingshi.

sentences.zh: 善政事，好發擿姦伏，盜賊稀發，吏人畏愛之。

translations.literal: He was skilled in administration, fond of exposing hidden villainy; bandits rarely arose; clerks and people feared and loved him.
translations.idiomatic: He ran an efficient government, dug out concealed crime, and kept banditry rare; officials and commoners alike respected and trusted him.

sentences.zh: 南陽太守鮑得上其理狀，遷宛陵令。

translations.literal: The governor of Nanyang, Bao De, submitted upward his record of good governance; he was transferred to be magistrate of Wanling.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Bao De of Nanyang reported his exemplary record, and Xiong was promoted to magistrate of Wanling.

sentences.zh: 永初三年，海賊張伯路等三千餘人，冠赤幘，服絳衣，自稱「將軍」，寇濱海九郡，殺二千石令長。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yongchu, the sea bandits Zhang Bolu and others, more than three thousand men, wore red kerchiefs and crimson clothing, called themselves "General," raided nine coastal commanderies, and killed governors and magistrates of two-thousand–picul rank.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 3 (109 CE), Zhang Bolu and over three thousand pirates donned red headbands and scarlet robes, styled themselves generals, ravaged nine seaboard commanderies, and murdered prefects and county magistrates.

sentences.zh: 初，遣侍御史龐雄督州郡兵擊之，伯路等乞降，尋復屯聚。

translations.literal: At first, Attendant Censor Pang Xiong was sent to supervise the troops of the provinces and commanderies to strike them; Bolu and the others begged to surrender, but soon gathered again.
translations.idiomatic: At first, censor Pang Xiong led provincial forces against them; Zhang Bolu's band asked to surrender, then reassembled.

sentences.zh: 明年，伯路復與平原劉文河等三百餘人稱「使者」。

translations.literal: The next year, Bolu again with three hundred-odd men such as Liu Wenhe of Pingyuan called themselves "envoys."
translations.idiomatic: The following year Zhang Bolu joined some three hundred men, including Liu Wenhe of Pingyuan, and they styled themselves imperial envoys.

sentences.zh: 攻厭次城，殺長吏，轉入高唐，燒官寺，出繫囚，渠帥皆稱「將軍」，共朝謁伯路。

translations.literal: They attacked Yanci city, killed the senior official, moved into Gaotang, burned government offices, released bound prisoners, and the chieftains all called themselves "General" and together paid court to Bolu.
translations.idiomatic: They stormed Yanci, murdered senior officers, swung into Gaotang, torched yamen offices, freed the jailed—the band leaders all took the title general and did homage to Zhang Bolu.

sentences.zh: 伯路冠五梁冠，佩印綬，黨眾浸盛。

translations.literal: Bolu wore a five-ridge official cap and bore seal and ribbon; the faction daily grew stronger.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bolu dressed in a five-ridge cap with seal and ribbons at his belt, and his following swelled.

sentences.zh: 乃遣御史中丞王宗持節發幽、冀諸郡兵，合數萬人，乃徵雄為青州刺史，與王宗并力討之。

translations.literal: Thereupon Vice Censor-in-chief Wang Zong was sent bearing credentials to mobilize the troops of the commanderies of You and Ji, totaling several tens of thousands; Xiong was then summoned to be governor of Qingzhou and with Wang Zong joined forces to attack them.
translations.idiomatic: The court ordered Vice Censor Wang Zong, staff in hand, to raise tens of thousands from Youzhou and Ji province; Fa Xiong was called to serve as governor of Qingzhou, and the two commanders campaigned together.

sentences.zh: 連戰破賊，斬首溺死者數百人，餘皆奔走，收器械財物甚眾。

translations.literal: In successive battles they defeated the bandits; several hundred were beheaded or drowned; the rest all fled; weapons and goods captured were very many.
translations.idiomatic: They won a string of engagements—hundreds of rebels were killed or drowned in the rout—and seized large quantities of arms and loot.

sentences.zh: 會赦詔到，賊猶以軍甲未解，不敢歸降。

translations.literal: When an amnesty edict arrived, the bandits still because armor was not laid aside did not dare return and surrender.
translations.idiomatic: An amnesty arrived, but the pirates had not yet shed their arms and dared not give themselves up.

sentences.zh: 於是王宗召刺史太守共議，皆以為當遂擊之。

translations.literal: Thereupon Wang Zong summoned the governors and grand administrators to deliberate together; all thought they ought immediately to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Zong called the provincial and prefectural officials to council; everyone favored pressing the attack.

sentences.zh: 雄曰：「不然。

translations.literal: Xiong said: "It is not so."
translations.idiomatic: Xiong disagreed.

sentences.zh: 兵，凶器；

translations.literal: Weapons are instruments of ill omen;
translations.idiomatic: Arms are tools of violence;

sentences.zh: 戰，危事。

translations.literal: warfare is a perilous affair.
translations.idiomatic: war is always perilous.

sentences.zh: 勇不可恃，勝不可必。

translations.literal: Bravery cannot be relied on; victory cannot be guaranteed.
translations.idiomatic: Courage alone is no guarantee, and no battle is a sure win.

sentences.zh: 賊若乘船浮海，深入遠島，攻之未易也。

translations.literal: If the bandits ride boats on the sea and go deep to distant islands, attacking them will not be easy.
translations.idiomatic: If they put to sea and hole up on distant islands, driving them out will be costly.

sentences.zh: 及有赦令，可且罷兵，以慰誘其心，埶必解散，然後圖之，可不戰而定也。

translations.literal: Moreover there is an amnesty edict; we can for the moment disband troops, to soothe and entice their hearts—the situation will certainly dissolve, then we can plan against them—can be settled without battle.
translations.idiomatic: We have an amnesty in hand; stand the army down for now, offer reassurance, and their cohesion will crack—then we can pick them off without another pitched battle.

sentences.zh: 」宗善其言，即罷兵。

translations.literal: " Zong approved his words and immediately disbanded the troops.
translations.idiomatic: " Wang Zong accepted the advice and ordered the forces stood down.

sentences.zh: 賊聞大喜，乃還所略人。

translations.literal: The bandits heard and greatly rejoiced; thereupon they returned the people they had carried off.
translations.idiomatic: The rebels welcomed the news and began releasing their captives.

sentences.zh: 而東萊郡兵獨未解甲，賊復驚恐，遁走遼東，止海島上。

translations.literal: But the troops of Donglai commandery alone had not removed armor; the bandits again were alarmed and frightened, fled to Liaodong, and stopped on sea islands.
translations.idiomatic: Only Donglai's troops kept their armor on; the pirates panicked again, fled to Liaodong, and camped on offshore islets.

sentences.zh: 五年春，乏食，復抄東萊閒，雄率郡兵擊破之，賊逃還遼東，遼東人李久等共斬平之，於是州界清靜。

translations.literal: In the spring of the fifth year, short of food, they again plundered the borders of Donglai; Xiong led commandery troops and defeated them; the bandits fled back to Liaodong; men of Liaodong such as Li Jiu together beheaded and pacified them—thereupon the province's borders were quiet.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of the fifth year they ran short of supplies and raided Donglai again; Xiong led local troops and broke them. The survivors fled to Liaodong, where locals led by Li Jiu killed the ringleaders and restored order; the province knew peace.

sentences.zh: 雄每行部，錄囚徒，察顏色，多得情偽，長吏不奉法者皆解印綬去。

translations.literal: Whenever Xiong toured his department, he recorded prisoners, examined their expressions, and often got the truth of falsehood; senior officials who did not observe the law all removed seal-ribbons and departed.
translations.idiomatic: On his inspection rounds he reviewed the jails, watched the prisoners' faces, and usually saw through lies; any senior officer who flouted the law was made to hand in his credentials and leave.

sentences.zh: 在州四年，遷南郡太守，斷獄省少，戶口益增。

translations.literal: After four years in the province, he was transferred to grand administrator of Nan commandery; legal cases were few, households daily increased.
translations.idiomatic: Four years as governor won him promotion to grand administrator of Nan, where he kept litigation light and the registered population grew.

sentences.zh: 郡濱帶江沔，又有雲夢藪澤，永初中，多虎狼之暴，前太守賞募張捕，反為所害者甚眾。

translations.literal: The commandery bordered the Yang and Mian rivers, and also had the Yunmeng marshlands; in the Yongchu period, there were many ravages of tigers and wolves; the previous grand administrator offered rewards and organized nets to catch them, but those harmed in return were very many.
translations.idiomatic: The commandery straddled the Han River and the Yunmeng marshes. In the Yongchu years (107–113) tigers and wolves ran riot; the previous administrator had set bounties and organized hunts, and more people died in the attempt than were saved.

sentences.zh: 雄乃移書屬縣曰：「凡虎狼之在山林，猶人之居城市。

translations.literal: Xiong thereupon sent a circular to the subordinate counties, saying: "In general, tigers and wolves in mountain forests are like people dwelling in cities."
translations.idiomatic: Xiong wrote to the counties: "Tigers in the hills are no more out of place than people in a town."

sentences.zh: 古者至化之世，猛獸不擾，皆由恩信寬澤，仁及飛走。

translations.literal: In antiquity, in an age of utmost transformation, fierce beasts did not harm—all was due to favor, trust, lenience, and moisture, benevolence reaching to flying and running things.
translations.idiomatic: In a truly well-governed age, wild beasts do not molest the people, because grace and good faith reach even the birds and beasts."

sentences.zh: 太守雖不德，敢忘斯義。

translations.literal: Though the grand administrator lacks virtue, how dare I forget this principle.
translations.idiomatic: I am no paragon, but I will not forget that duty."

sentences.zh: 記到，其毀壞檻阱，不得妄捕山林。

translations.literal: When this record arrives, destroy cages and pit-traps; do not recklessly capture in mountain forests.
translations.idiomatic: On receipt of this order, tear down every trap and snare, and do not hunt the hills without cause."

sentences.zh: 」是後虎害稍息，人以獲安。

translations.literal: " After this, tiger harm gradually ceased and people obtained peace.
translations.idiomatic: " After that, tiger depredations eased and the people could live without fear.

sentences.zh: 在郡數歲，歲常豐稔。

translations.literal: In the commandery for several years, the years were always abundantly ripe.
translations.idiomatic: Year after year in the commandery the harvests were full.

sentences.zh: 元初中卒官。

translations.literal: In the early Yuanhe period, he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in the early Yuanhe era (114–119 CE).

sentences.zh: 子真，在《逸人傳》。

translations.literal: His son Zhen is in the "Biographies of Recluses."
translations.idiomatic: His son Fa Zhen is treated in the chapter on recluses.

sentences.zh: 滕撫字叔輔，北海劇人也。

translations.literal: Teng Fu, courtesy name Shufu, was a man of Ju in Beihai.
translations.idiomatic: Teng Fu, styled Shufu, hailed from Ju county in Beihai commandery.

sentences.zh: 初仕州郡，稍遷為涿令，有文武才用。

translations.literal: He first served in province and commandery, was gradually promoted to magistrate of Zhuo, and had civil and military talent and usefulness.
translations.idiomatic: He rose through provincial posts to magistrate of Zhuo, showing talent both in administration and in arms.

sentences.zh: 太守以其能，委任郡職，兼領六縣。

translations.literal: The grand administrator because of his ability entrusted him with commandery duties and concurrently led six counties.
translations.idiomatic: The governor, impressed by his ability, gave him broad duties over six counties.

sentences.zh: 風政修明，流愛于人，在事七年，道不拾遺。

translations.literal: Customs and governance were cultivated and clear; love flowed to people; during seven years in office, things dropped on the road were not picked up.
translations.idiomatic: His rule was orderly and humane; for seven years lost goods stayed where they fell.

sentences.zh: 順帝末，揚、徐盜賊群起，磐牙連歲。

translations.literal: Near the end of Emperor Shun's reign, bandits of Yang and Xu rose in crowds, tangled like teeth for successive years.
translations.idiomatic: Late in Emperor Shun's reign banditry erupted in Yangzhou and Xuzhou and dragged on year after year.

sentences.zh: 建康元年，九江范容、周生等相聚反亂，屯據歷陽，為江淮巨患，遣御史中丞馮緄將兵督揚州刺史尹燿、九江太守鄧顯討之。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jiankang, Fan Rong and Zhou Sheng and others of Jiujiang gathered in rebellion, encamped and held Liyang, became a great peril of the Yang and Huai; Vice Censor-in-chief Feng Gun was sent leading troops to supervise Governor of Yangzhou Yin Yao and Grand Administrator of Jiujiang Deng Xian to attack them.
translations.idiomatic: In Jiankang 1 (144 CE), Fan Rong, Zhou Sheng, and others in Jiujiang rebelled, seized Liyang, and threatened the entire Yang–Huai region. The court dispatched Vice Censor Feng Gun with troops to coordinate Governor Yin Yao of Yangzhou and Prefect Deng Xian of Jiujiang against them.

sentences.zh: 燿、顯軍敗，為賊所殺。

translations.literal: Yao's and Xian's armies were defeated; they were killed by the bandits.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Yao and Deng Xian were routed and killed by the rebels.

sentences.zh: 又陰陵人徐鳳、馬勉等復寇郡縣，殺略吏人。

translations.literal: Moreover men of Yinling, Xu Feng and Ma Mian and others again raided commanderies and counties, killing and plundering clerks and people.
translations.idiomatic: From Yinling, Xu Feng and Ma Mian attacked counties again, killing officials and looting the countryside.

sentences.zh: 鳳衣絳衣，帶黑綬，稱「無上將軍」，勉皮冠黃衣，帶玉印，稱「黃帝」，築營於當塗山中。

translations.literal: Feng wore crimson clothing and a black ribbon, calling himself "Supreme General"; Mian wore a leather cap and yellow clothing, bore a jade seal, called himself "Yellow Emperor," and built a camp in the mountains of Dangtu.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Feng dressed in scarlet with a black official ribbon and styled himself Supreme General; Ma Mian wore a leather cap, yellow robes, and a jade seal and proclaimed himself the Yellow Emperor, fortifying a camp in the Dangtu hills.

sentences.zh: 乃建年號，置百官，遣別帥黃虎攻沒合肥。

translations.literal: Thereupon they established an era name, set up the hundred offices, and sent a separate commander Huang Hu to attack and overrun Hefei.
translations.idiomatic: They adopted a reign title, appointed a full bureaucracy, and detached Huang Hu to take Hefei.

sentences.zh: 明年，廣陵賊張嬰等復聚眾數千人反，據廣陵。

translations.literal: The next year, bandits of Guangling such as Zhang Ying again gathered a crowd of several thousand rebels and held Guangling.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Zhang Ying of Guangling rallied thousands and seized the commandery seat.

sentences.zh: 朝廷博求將帥，三公舉撫有文武才，拜為九江都尉，與中郎將趙序助馮緄合州郡兵數萬人共討之。

translations.literal: The court broadly sought generals; the Three Excellencies recommended Fu as having civil and military talent; he was appointed Commandant of Jiujiang, and with General of the Household Zhao Xu assisted Feng Gun in combining the troops of provinces and commanderies, several tens of thousands, jointly to attack them.
translations.idiomatic: The court cast about for commanders; the Three Excellencies nominated Teng Fu for his civil and military gifts. He received the post of commandant of Jiujiang and, with General of the Household Zhao Xu under Feng Gun, led tens of thousands of provincial troops against the rebels.

sentences.zh: 又廣開賞募，錢、邑各有差。

translations.literal: Moreover rewards for recruitment were broadly opened; money and fiefs each had gradations.
translations.idiomatic: Generous bounties were posted—cash rewards and noble fiefs scaled by rank.

sentences.zh: 梁太后慮群賊屯結，諸將不能制，又議遣太尉李固。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Liang worried that the mass bandits massed and joined, the generals could not control them, and again deliberated sending Grand Commandant Li Gu.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Liang feared the rebels were digging in beyond the generals' reach and considered sending Grand Commandant Li Gu as well.

sentences.zh: 未及行，會撫等進擊，大破之，斬馬勉、范容、周生等千五百級，徐鳳遂將餘眾攻燒東城縣。

translations.literal: Before he could go, Fu and others advanced and attacked, greatly defeated them, beheaded Ma Mian, Fan Rong, Zhou Sheng and others to the count of fifteen hundred; Xu Feng then led the remaining host to attack and burn Dongcheng county.
translations.idiomatic: Before Li Gu could march, Teng Fu attacked and routed the enemy, taking fifteen hundred heads including Ma Mian, Fan Rong, and Zhou Sheng. Xu Feng escaped with the survivors and torched Dongcheng county.

sentences.zh: 下邳人謝安應募，率其宗親設伏擊鳳，斬之，封安為平鄉侯，邑三千戶。

translations.literal: Xie An of Xiapi responded to recruitment, led his clan and kin to lay an ambush and strike Feng, beheaded him; An was enfeoffed as marquis of Pingxiang with three thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Xie An of Xiapi answered the call, ambushed Xu Feng with his kinsmen, and killed him. The court ennobled him as marquis of Pingxiang with a fief of three thousand households.

sentences.zh: 拜撫中郎將，督揚徐二州事。

translations.literal: Fu was appointed General of the Household and supervised the affairs of Yang and Xu two provinces.
translations.idiomatic: Teng Fu was promoted to general of the household with authority over Yangzhou and Xuzhou.

sentences.zh: 撫復進擊張嬰，斬獲千餘人。

translations.literal: Fu again advanced and attacked Zhang Ying, beheaded and captured more than a thousand.
translations.idiomatic: He pressed on against Zhang Ying and killed or captured over a thousand men.

sentences.zh: 趙序坐畏懦不進，詐增首級，徵還棄市。

translations.literal: Zhao Xu sat guilty of cowardice and not advancing, falsely increasing head-counts; he was recalled and executed in the market.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xu was convicted of hanging back, inflating body counts, and was dragged back to the capital and executed in the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 又歷陽賊華孟自稱「黑帝」，攻九江，殺郡守。

translations.literal: Moreover the bandit of Liyang, Hua Meng, called himself "Black Emperor," attacked Jiujiang, and killed the grand administrator.
translations.idiomatic: Hua Meng of Liyang styled himself the Black Emperor, struck Jiujiang, and murdered the prefect.

sentences.zh: 撫乘勝進擊，破之，斬孟等三千八百級，虜獲七百餘人，牛馬財物不可勝筭。

translations.literal: Fu rode victory and advanced to attack, defeated them, beheaded Meng and others to three thousand eight hundred levels, captured more than seven hundred; oxen, horses, and goods could not be counted.
translations.idiomatic: Teng Fu followed up his victory, shattered Hua Meng's force, and counted 3,800 enemy dead with 700 prisoners; livestock and booty were beyond reckoning.

sentences.zh: 於是東南悉平，振旅而還。

translations.literal: Thereupon the southeast was entirely pacified; he shook the troops and returned.
translations.idiomatic: The southeast was finally quiet; he marched his army home.

sentences.zh: 以撫為左馮翊，除一子為郎。

translations.literal: Fu was made Administrator of the Left to Fengyi; one son was appointed gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: The court named him governor of Left Fengyi and enrolled one son as a court gentleman.

sentences.zh: 撫所得賞賜，盡分於麾下。

translations.literal: What Fu obtained in rewards and gifts he entirely divided among those under his banner.
translations.idiomatic: He shared every bonus and gift with his troops.

sentences.zh: 性方直，不交權埶，宦官懷忿。

translations.literal: His nature was upright and straight; he did not associate with powerful interests; eunuchs harbored resentment.
translations.idiomatic: He was bluntly honest and kept clear of powerful factions, which earned him the enmity of the eunuchs.

sentences.zh: 及論功當封，太尉胡廣時錄尚書事，承旨奏黜撫，天下怨之。

translations.literal: When merit was discussed and he ought to have been enfeoffed, Grand Commandant Hu Guang at the time held the office of recording imperial secretary affairs; receiving intent he memorialized to dismiss Fu—the realm resented it.
translations.idiomatic: When honors were due for his victories, Grand Commandant Hu Guang—then directing the Secretariat—carried out an order to strip Teng Fu of recognition, and public opinion turned bitterly against the court.

sentences.zh: 卒於家。

translations.literal: He died at home.
translations.idiomatic: He died at home.

sentences.zh: 馮緄字鴻卿，巴郡宕渠人也，少學春秋、司馬兵法。

translations.literal: Feng Gun, courtesy name Hongqing, was a man of Dangqu in Ba commandery; in youth he studied the Spring and Autumn Annals and Sima's Art of War.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Gun, styled Hongqing, came from Dangqu in Ba commandery and as a young man studied the *Spring and Autumn* and the military treatises.

sentences.zh: 父煥，安帝時為幽州刺史，疾忌姦惡，數致其罪。

translations.literal: His father Huan, in the time of Emperor An, was governor of Youzhou; he detested wickedness and wrongdoing and repeatedly secured convictions against offenders.
translations.idiomatic: His father Feng Huan served as inspector of Youzhou under Emperor An, pursued wrongdoing zealously, and repeatedly secured convictions.

sentences.zh: 時玄菟太守姚光亦失人和。

translations.literal: At that time the grand administrator of Xuantu, Yao Guang, also lost harmony among people.
translations.idiomatic: Yao Guang, prefect of Xuantu, had likewise alienated those under him.

sentences.zh: 建光元年，怨者乃詐作璽書譴責煥、光，賜以歐刀。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianguang, those who resented then forged a sealed edict reproaching Huan and Guang, granting them the executioner's blade.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianguang 1 (121 CE), enemies forged an imperial rescript rebuking Feng Huan and Yao Guang and sentencing them to suicide with the court blade.

sentences.zh: 又下遼東都尉龐奮使速行刑，奮即斬光收煥。

translations.literal: Moreover orders went down to Commandant of Liaodong Pang Fen to hasten execution; Fen immediately beheaded Guang and seized Huan.
translations.idiomatic: Liaodong commandant Pang Fen was told to act at once; he executed Yao Guang on the spot and took Feng Huan into custody.

sentences.zh: 煥欲自殺，緄疑詔文有異，止煥曰：「大人在州，志欲去惡，實無它故，必是凶人妄詐，規肆姦毒。

translations.literal: Huan wished to kill himself; Gun suspected the edict text had anomaly, stopped Huan and said: "The great man in the province, his aim wished to remove evil—truly there is no other reason; it must be vicious men falsely deceiving, plotting to unleash villainous poison."
translations.idiomatic: Feng Huan was about to kill himself when his son, doubting the edict, stopped him. "In office you have sought only to root out evil," Feng Gun said; "there is no other cause—this must be a forgery by someone scheming against you."

sentences.zh: 願以事自上，甘罪無晚。

translations.literal: I wish to take the matter upward myself—willing to suffer guilt is not late."
translations.idiomatic: Petition the throne first; if we are wrong, we can face punishment then."

sentences.zh: 」煥從其言，上書自訟，果詐者所為，徵奮抵罪。

translations.literal: " Huan followed his words, submitted a memorial self-accusing; it was indeed the doings of the forger; Pang Fen was summoned to answer for guilt.
translations.idiomatic: " Feng Huan took the advice, appealed to the emperor, and exposed the fraud; Pang Fen was recalled and punished.

sentences.zh: 會煥病死獄中，帝愍之，賜煥、光錢各十萬，以子為郎中。

translations.literal: When Huan fell ill and died in prison, the emperor pitied him, granted Huan and Guang each one hundred thousand cash, and appointed the son gentleman of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Huan died in prison before he could be cleared; moved with pity, the emperor awarded his family and Yao Guang's one hundred thousand cash each and enrolled Feng Gun as a gentleman of the palace.

sentences.zh: 緄由是知名。

translations.literal: Gun from this became known.
translations.idiomatic: That incident made Feng Gun's name.

sentences.zh: 家富好施，賑赴窮急，為州里所歸愛。

translations.literal: The family was wealthy and fond of giving; they relieved and rushed to the poor and urgent; they were returned to and loved by the province and village.
translations.idiomatic: The household was rich and open-handed, quick to aid neighbors in want, and widely esteemed in the region.

sentences.zh: 初舉孝廉，七遷為廣漢屬國都尉，徵拜御史中丞。

translations.literal: At first he was recommended as Filially Pious and Incorrupt; after seven promotions he became Commandant of the Dependent State of Guanghan; he was summoned and appointed Vice Censor-in-chief.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended first as filially pious and incorrupt, he rose through seven posts to commandant of the Guanghan dependent state, then received a summons to serve as vice censor-in-chief.

sentences.zh: 順帝末，以緄持節督揚州諸郡軍事，與中郎將滕撫擊破群賊，遷隴西太守。

translations.literal: Near the end of Emperor Shun's reign, because Gun held the imperial credentials he supervised military affairs in the commanderies of Yangzhou; with General of the Household Teng Fu he crushed the mass bandits and was transferred to grand administrator of Longxi.
translations.idiomatic: Late in Emperor Shun's reign he carried the staff of authority over Yangzhou's forces and, with General Teng Fu, broke the rebel armies before moving on to grand administrator of Longxi.

sentences.zh: 後鮮卑寇邊，以緄為遼東太守，曉喻降集，虜皆弭散。

translations.literal: Later the Xianbei raided the frontier; Gun was appointed grand administrator of Liaodong; he enlightened and persuaded them to surrender and gather; the raiders all dispersed quietly.
translations.idiomatic: When the Xianbei struck again, he governed Liaodong, talked the tribes into submission, and they melted away without further fighting.

sentences.zh: 徵拜京兆尹，轉司隸校尉，所在立威刑。

translations.literal: He was summoned as metropolitan governor of Jingzhao, transferred to colonel director of retainers; wherever he went he established awe-inspiring punishments.
translations.idiomatic: He was called to the capital as governor of Jingzhao, then became colonel director of retainers, earning a reputation for stern justice wherever he served.

sentences.zh: 遷廷尉、太常。

translations.literal: He was promoted to commandant of justice and minister of ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to commandant of justice and minister of ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 時長沙蠻寇益陽，屯聚積久，至延熹五年，眾轉盛，而零陵蠻賊復反應之，合二萬餘人，攻燒城郭，殺傷長吏。

translations.literal: At that time the Man of Changsha raided Yiyang, encamped and gathered for a long time; by the fifth year of Yanxi the host grew stronger, and the Man bandits of Lingling in turn rose in response; joining to more than twenty thousand, they attacked and burned city walls, killed and wounded senior officials.
translations.idiomatic: The Changsha tribes had been raiding Yiyang for some time, and by Yanxi 5 (162 CE) their numbers surged. The Lingling tribes joined in, and more than twenty thousand men burned towns and cut down magistrates.

sentences.zh: 又武陵蠻夷悉反，寇掠江陵閒，荊州刺史劉度、南郡太守李肅並奔走荊南，皆沒。

translations.literal: Moreover the Man and Yi of Wuling all rebelled, raided and plundered around Jiangling; Governor of Jingzhou Liu Du and Grand Administrator of Nanjun Li Su together fled south of Jing—all perished.
translations.idiomatic: Every tribe in Wuling rose as well, sweeping the Jiangling region. Inspector Liu Du of Jingzhou and Nanjun's Li Su bolted for the south of the province and were lost with their men.

sentences.zh: 於是拜緄為車騎將軍，將兵十餘萬討之，詔策緄曰：「蠻夷猾夏，久不討攝，各焚都城，蹈籍官人。

translations.literal: Thereupon Gun was appointed General of Chariots and Cavalry, leading more than a hundred thousand troops to attack them. The edict enfeoffing Gun said: "The Man and Yi vex the Hua, long unrestful and unchastised; each has burned the capital city, trampled and trampled the officials."
translations.idiomatic: Feng Gun was made General of Chariots and Cavalry at the head of a host of more than a hundred thousand. The mandate read: "The southern tribes have long afflicted the heartland, evading punishment; they have torched our cities and trampled our officials."

sentences.zh: 州郡將吏，死職之臣，相逐奔竄，曾不反顧，可愧言也。

translations.literal: The generals and clerks of provinces and commanderies, ministers who should die in office, flee in mutual pursuit, never looking back—words of shame."
translations.idiomatic: Our officers, who should stand their posts, are running in disorder without a backward glance—this is a disgrace."

sentences.zh: 將軍素有威猛，是以擢授六師。

translations.literal: The general from of old has had awe and might; for this he is raised to command the six hosts."
translations.idiomatic: You have long been known for your vigor, and for that we entrust you with the main army."

sentences.zh: 前代陳湯、馮、傅之徒，以寡擊眾，郅支、夜郎、樓蘭之戎，頭懸都街，衛、霍北征，功列金石，是皆將軍所究覽也。

translations.literal: In former times Chen Tang, Feng, Fu, and the like, with the few struck the many; the Rong of Zhizhi, Yelang, and Loulan—heads hung in the capital streets; Wei and Huo campaigned north, merit carved in metal and stone—these are all those the general has thoroughly surveyed."
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang, Feng Fengshi, Fu Jiezi, and others defeated vast armies with small forces; barbarian heads from Zhizhi, Yelang, and Loulan hung in the marketplace; Wei Qing and Huo Qubing marched north and earned inscriptions on bronze—all examples you know well."

sentences.zh: 今非將軍，誰與修復前跡？

translations.literal: Now without the general, who shall together restore the traces of old?"
translations.idiomatic: Who but you can renew those deeds of old?"

sentences.zh: 進赴之宜，權時之策，將軍一之，出郊之事，不復內御。

translations.literal: The fitness of advance and rush, the stratagems suited to the hour—the general alone decides; matters once beyond the outer suburbs, no more inner control."
translations.idiomatic: Tactical decisions in the field are yours alone; once the army has crossed the walls, the court will not second-guess you."

sentences.zh: 已命有司祖于國門。

translations.literal: We have already ordered the officials to perform the road sacrifice at the capital gate."
translations.idiomatic: The ministers have already sacrificed at the city gate to speed your departure."

sentences.zh: 詩不云乎：『進厥虎臣，闞如虓虎，敷敦淮濆，仍執醜虜。

translations.literal: Does not the Odes say: 'Advance those tiger-like ministers, fierce like roaring tigers; spread forth along the Huai's banks, still capturing ugly captives.'
translations.idiomatic: The *Classic of Poetry* says: "Advance your tiger-like ministers, fierce as roaring tigers; range the banks of the Huai and seize the foe."

sentences.zh: 』將軍其勉之！」

translations.literal: ' Strive, General!"
translations.idiomatic: Strive, General!"

sentences.zh: 時天下飢饉，帑藏虛盡，每出征伐，常減公卿奉祿，假王侯租賦，前後所遣將帥，宦官輒陷以折耗軍資，往往抵罪。

translations.literal: At the time the realm was in famine, the treasuries were empty; whenever there was a campaign, they regularly reduced the salaries of dukes and ministers, borrowed the rent and tax of princes and marquises; the generals and commanders sent out before and after, the eunuchs always entrapped on the charge of depleting military funds—often they answered for crime.
translations.idiomatic: Famine had emptied the vaults, and every campaign meant slashing pay for the high officials and seizing princely revenues. Eunuchs habitually accused returning commanders of embezzling military funds, and many were ruined.

sentences.zh: 緄性烈直，不行賄賂，懼為所中，乃上疏曰：「埶得容姦，伯夷可疑；

translations.literal: Gun by nature was fierce and straight, did not practice bribes, feared to be hit by them, and therefore submitted a memorial, saying: "If power can shelter wickedness, then Boyi is suspect;
translations.idiomatic: Feng Gun was blunt and took no bribes, yet he feared a eunuch's frame. He wrote: "If favoritism can make a villain of a worthy, even Boyi would look guilty;

sentences.zh: 苟曰無猜，盜跖可信。

translations.literal: if it is said there is no suspicion, then Robber Zhi is trustworthy."
translations.idiomatic: if you insist there is no bias, then even Robber Zhi would pass for honest."

sentences.zh: 故樂羊陳功，文侯示以謗書。

translations.literal: Therefore when Yue Yang presented his merit, Marquis Wen showed him the slanderous letters."
translations.idiomatic: That is why Marquis Wen of Wei answered Yue Yang's report of victory by producing the letters that had slandered him."

sentences.zh: 願請中常侍一人監軍財費。

translations.literal: I beg to request one regular palace attendant to supervise military funds and expenses."
translations.idiomatic: I ask that one eunuch be assigned to audit the army's accounts."

sentences.zh: 」尚書朱穆奏緄以財自嫌，失大臣之節。

translations.literal: " Director of the secretariat Zhu Mu memorialized that Gun, by the funds, brought suspicion on himself, and lost a great minister's integrity.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Mu countered that Feng Gun had impugned his own integrity with this talk of money and fallen short of a senior minister's bearing.

sentences.zh: 有詔勿劾。

translations.literal: There was an edict: do not impeach.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ordered that no action be taken against him.

sentences.zh: 緄軍至長沙，賊聞，悉詣營道乞降。

translations.literal: When Gun's army reached Changsha, the bandits heard and all went to Yingdao to beg to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: When Feng Gun's host reached Changsha, the rebels learned of it and flocked to Yingdao to surrender.

sentences.zh: 進擊武陵蠻夷，斬首四千餘級，受降十餘萬人，荊州平定。

translations.literal: He advanced and struck the Man and Yi of Wuling, beheaded more than four thousand levels, received the surrender of more than a hundred thousand people—Jingzhou was pacified.
translations.idiomatic: He drove into Wuling: four thousand enemy dead, more than a hundred thousand surrendered, and Jingzhou was quiet.

sentences.zh: 詔書賜錢一億，固讓不受。

translations.literal: An edict granted one hundred million cash; he firmly declined and did not accept.
translations.idiomatic: The court offered him a hundred million cash; he refused repeatedly.

sentences.zh: 振旅還京師，推功於從事中郎應奉，薦以為司隸校尉，而上書乞骸骨，朝廷不許。

translations.literal: He shook the troops and returned to the capital, pushed merit onto staff officer Ying Feng, recommended him to be colonel director of retainers, and submitted a memorial begging for his old bones; the court did not permit.
translations.idiomatic: He led the army back, gave the credit to his aide Ying Feng, and recommended Feng for colonel director of retainers, then asked to retire; the court said no.

sentences.zh: 監軍使者張敞承宦官旨，奏緄將傅婢二人戎服自隨，又輒於江陵刻石紀功，請下吏案理。

translations.literal: The overseer of the army, messenger Zhang Chang, following eunuch intent, memorialized that Gun had two concubine attendants in military garb follow him, and also had at Jiangling carved stone to record merit; he asked to hand down to officials for case review.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chang, the army monitor, at the eunuchs' bidding accused Feng Gun of taking two maids in military dress on campaign and of carving a victory stele at Jiangling without leave. He asked the judiciary to open a case.

sentences.zh: 尚書令黃雋奏議，以為罪無正法，不合致糾。

translations.literal: Secretariat director Huang Jun submitted deliberation, thinking the crime had no correct law, not fitting to bring impeachment.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Jun, head of the Secretariat, argued that no statute covered the alleged offenses, so prosecution was unwarranted.

sentences.zh: 會長沙賊復起，攻桂陽、武陵，緄以軍還盜賊復發，策免。

translations.literal: It happened that Changsha bandits rose again, attacked Guiyang and Wuling; Gun, because the army had returned and bandits had broken out again, was removed by edict.
translations.idiomatic: Just then revolt flared again in Changsha, hitting Guiyang and Wuling. Because trouble returned as soon as his army withdrew, the emperor dismissed him by edict.

sentences.zh: 頃之，拜將作大匠，轉河南尹。

translations.literal: Before long, he was appointed Superintendent of Construction, transferred to metropolitan governor of Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Soon he became superintendent of imperial workshops, then governor of Henan commandery.

sentences.zh: 上言「舊典，中官子弟不得為牧人職」，帝不納。

translations.literal: He submitted upward saying "by old precedent, sons and younger brothers of palace officials may not hold shepherd offices"; the emperor did not adopt.
translations.idiomatic: He urged that by tradition eunuchs' kin must not hold provincial posts; the emperor ignored him.

sentences.zh: 復為廷尉。

translations.literal: Again he became commandant of justice.
translations.idiomatic: He was again named commandant of justice.

sentences.zh: 時山陽太守單遷以罪繫獄，緄考致其死。

translations.literal: At the time the grand administrator of Shanyang, Shan Qian, was bound in prison for crime; Gun questioned him and brought about his death.
translations.idiomatic: When Shan Qian, prefect of Shanyang, was jailed on charges, Feng Gun's interrogation killed him.

sentences.zh: 遷，故車騎將軍單超之弟，中官相黨，遂共誹章誣緄，坐與司隸校尉李膺、大司農劉祐俱輸左校。

translations.literal: Qian was the younger brother of the former General of Chariots and Cavalry Shan Chao; the palace officials formed a faction and together slandered with memorials and falsely accused Gun; by crime together with Colonel Director of Retainers Li Ying and Minister of Finance Liu You all were sent to the Left Craftsmen.
translations.idiomatic: Shan Qian was the younger brother of the late General Shan Chao; the eunuchs closed ranks and framed Feng Gun. He was sentenced to hard labor at the Left Craftsmen prison alongside Li Ying and Liu You.

sentences.zh: 應奉上疏理緄等，得免。

translations.literal: Ying Feng submitted a memorial to straighten out Gun and the others; they obtained release.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Feng petitioned on their behalf and won their release.

sentences.zh: 後拜屯騎校尉，復為廷尉，卒於官。

translations.literal: Later he was appointed colonel of the garrison cavalry, again became commandant of justice, and died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He later served as colonel of garrison cavalry and again as commandant of justice, dying in office.

sentences.zh: 緄弟允，清白有孝行，能理尚書，善推步之術。

translations.literal: Gun's younger brother Yun, pure and white, had filial conduct, was able to manage the secretariat, was good at the art of calendrical calculation.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Gun's brother Feng Yun was known for integrity and filial piety, could run the imperial secretariat, and was skilled in astronomy and calendrics.

sentences.zh: 拜降虜校尉，終於家。

translations.literal: He was appointed colonel who subdues the Hu and ended at home.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to colonel who subdues the barbarians and died at home.

sentences.zh: 度尚字博平，山陽湖陸人也。

translations.literal: Du Shang, courtesy name Boping, was a man of Hulu in Shanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang, styled Boping, came from Hulu in Shanyang commandery.

sentences.zh: 家貧，不修學行，不為鄉里所推舉。

translations.literal: The family was poor; he did not cultivate learning and conduct; he was not recommended by village and district.
translations.idiomatic: His family was poor, he had little schooling, and local worthies did not recommend him.

sentences.zh: 積困窮，乃為宦者同郡侯覽視田，得為郡上計吏，拜郎中，除上虞長。

translations.literal: Accumulating distress and poverty, he then for the eunuch of the same commandery Hou Lan inspected fields, was able to become the commandery's tribute clerk, was appointed gentleman of the palace, removed as magistrate of Shangyu.
translations.idiomatic: Hard up, he took work surveying fields for the eunuch Hou Lan of his commandery, parlayed that into a post as tribute clerk, received appointment as a gentleman of the palace, and became magistrate of Shangyu.

sentences.zh: 為政嚴峻，明於發擿姦非，吏人謂之神明。

translations.literal: His governance was stern and severe; he was clear at exposing hidden villainy and wrong; clerks and people called him spirit-like bright.
translations.idiomatic: He ruled with an iron hand and exposed crime with uncanny precision; officials and commoners called him uncanny.

sentences.zh: 遷文安令，遇時疾疫，穀貴人飢，尚開倉稟給，營救疾者，百姓蒙其濟。

translations.literal: He was transferred to magistrate of Wen'an; encountering seasonal epidemic at the time, grain was expensive and people hungry; Shang opened granaries and issued relief, managed rescue of the sick; the hundred surnames received his succor.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted to Wen'an, he faced plague and famine; he opened the granaries, fed the starving, and cared for the sick—his people owed him their lives.

sentences.zh: 時冀州刺史朱穆行部，見尚甚奇之。

translations.literal: At the time Governor of Ji province Zhu Mu toured his department; seeing Shang he greatly marveled at him.
translations.idiomatic: When Inspector Zhu Mu of Ji province made his rounds, he took note of Du Shang with admiration.

sentences.zh: 延熹五年，長沙、零陵賊合七八千人，自稱「將軍」，入桂陽、蒼梧、南海、交阯，交阯刺史及蒼梧太守望風逃奔，二郡皆沒。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Yanxi, Changsha and Lingling bandits joined to seven or eight thousand men, called themselves "General," entered Guiyang, Cangwu, Nanhai, and Jiaozhi; the governor of Jiaozhi and the grand administrator of Cangwu fled at the wind; two commanderies were both lost.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanxi 5 (162 CE) seven or eight thousand rebels from Changsha and Lingling took the title general and swept through Guiyang, Cangwu, Nanhai, and Jiaozhi. The Jiaozhi inspector and Cangwu prefect fled without a fight and both commanderies fell.

sentences.zh: 遣御史中丞盛修募兵討之，不能剋。

translations.literal: Vice Censor-in-chief Sheng Xiu was sent to recruit troops and attack them; he could not overcome.
translations.idiomatic: Vice Censor Sheng Xiu raised troops against them but failed to win.

sentences.zh: 豫章艾縣人六百餘人，應募而不得賞直，怨恚，遂反，焚燒長沙郡縣，寇益陽，殺縣令，眾漸盛。

translations.literal: More than six hundred men of Ai county in Yuzhang, having responded to recruitment but not obtained reward payment, resented and hated; thereupon they rebelled, burned Changsha commandery and counties, raided Yiyang, killed the county magistrate—the host gradually grew strong.
translations.idiomatic: Some six hundred recruits from Ai county in Yuzhang had enlisted but never been paid; they revolted, torched Changsha, struck Yiyang, killed the magistrate, and swelled in numbers.

sentences.zh: 又遣謁者馬睦，督荊州刺史劉度擊之，軍敗，睦、度奔走。

translations.literal: Moreover messenger Ma Mu was sent to supervise Governor of Jingzhou Liu Du to attack them; the army was defeated; Mu and Du fled.
translations.idiomatic: The court sent Ma Mu to oversee Inspector Liu Du of Jingzhou; their force was routed and both men ran.

sentences.zh: 桓帝詔公卿舉任代劉度者，尚書朱穆舉尚，自右校令擢為荊州刺史。

translations.literal: Emperor Huan ordered dukes and ministers to recommend one fit to replace Liu Du; Director of the secretariat Zhu Mu recommended Shang; from Right Craftsmen commandant he was raised to governor of Jingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan asked his ministers for a successor to Liu Du; Zhu Mu nominated Du Shang, who jumped from commandant of the right craftsmen to inspector of Jingzhou.

sentences.zh: 尚躬率部曲，與同勞逸，廣募雜種諸蠻夷，明設購賞，進擊，大破之，降者數萬人。

translations.literal: Shang personally led his host, sharing labor and ease with them; broadly recruited miscellaneous sorts of the various Man and Yi; clearly set up rewards for capture; advanced and attacked, greatly defeated them; those who surrendered were several tens of thousands.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang led from the front, shared every hardship, recruited widely among the southern tribes, posted clear bounties, and smashed the rebels—tens of thousands surrendered.

sentences.zh: 桂陽宿賊渠帥卜陽、潘鴻等畏尚威烈，徙入山谷。

translations.literal: The old bandit chieftains of Guiyang, Bu Yang, Pan Hong and others feared Shang's awesome fierceness and moved into mountain valleys.
translations.idiomatic: The veteran rebel leaders Bu Yang and Pan Hong of Guiyang, fearful of Du Shang's reputation, withdrew into the hills.

sentences.zh: 尚窮追數百里，遂入南海，破其三屯，多獲珍寶。

translations.literal: Shang pursued exhaustively several hundred li, then entered Nanhai, broke their three encampments, and obtained many precious treasures.
translations.idiomatic: He chased them for hundreds of li into Nanhai commandery, overran three stockades, and took rich spoils.

sentences.zh: 而陽、鴻等黨眾猶盛，尚欲擊之，而士卒驕富，莫有鬥志。

translations.literal: But Yang's, Hong's, and the like faction host was still strong; Shang wished to strike them, but the soldiers were arrogant and rich, none had fighting will.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Bu Yang and Pan Hong still commanded a large following. Du Shang meant to finish them, but his men had grown complacent and rich and would not fight.

sentences.zh: 尚計緩之則不戰，逼之必逃亡，乃宣言卜陽、潘鴻作賊十年，習於攻守，今兵寡少，未易可進，當須諸郡所發悉至，爾乃并力攻之。

translations.literal: Shang calculated that if he eased them they would not fight, if he pressed them they would certainly flee; thereupon he proclaimed that Bu Yang and Pan Hong had been bandits ten years, practiced in attack and defense; now troops were few and scant, not easy to advance—it must wait until what the various commanderies had dispatched all arrived, only then join forces to attack them.
translations.idiomatic: He reasoned that slack pressure would mean no battle, while a tight siege would only drive the enemy off. He announced that Bu Yang and Pan Hong had ten years' experience in war, that his own force was too small for a rush assault, and that he would wait until every allied column arrived before striking.

sentences.zh: 申令軍中，恣聽射獵。

translations.literal: He issued orders in the army, freely permitting hunting.
translations.idiomatic: He then declared open hunting for the troops.

sentences.zh: 兵士喜悅，大小皆相與從禽。

translations.literal: The soldiers were pleased and joyful; great and small all together followed game.
translations.idiomatic: The men cheered and officers and ranks alike rode out after game.

sentences.zh: 尚乃密使所親客潛焚其營，珍積皆盡。

translations.literal: Shang thereupon secretly ordered his intimate retainers to secretly burn their camps; precious stores were all exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang secretly sent trusted agents to burn the army's own baggage train and destroy its hoarded loot.

sentences.zh: 獵者來還，莫不泣涕。

translations.literal: The hunters came back; none did not weep tears.
translations.idiomatic: When the hunting parties returned and saw the ashes, every man wept.

sentences.zh: 尚人人慰勞，深自咎責，因曰：「卜陽等財寶足富數世，諸卿但不并力耳。

translations.literal: Shang consoled each person, deeply blamed himself, and therefore said: "Bu Yang and the like's treasures and jewels are enough to enrich several generations—you gentlemen only did not join forces."
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang went among them, blamed himself bitterly, and said, "Bu Yang sits on enough loot to enrich generations—we failed only because we would not pull together."

sentences.zh: 所亡少少，何足介意！

translations.literal: What was lost was little little—how worth minding!"
translations.idiomatic: What we lost today is trifling—don't dwell on it!"

sentences.zh: 」眾聞咸憤踊，尚敕令秣馬蓐食，明旦，徑赴賊屯。

translations.literal: " The crowd hearing all were stirred and leaped; Shang ordered feed horses mat food; next dawn, went straight to the bandit encampment.
translations.idiomatic: " The troops boiled with fighting spirit. Du Shang ordered grain bagged for the horses and a meal wrapped for each man; at dawn he marched straight on the rebel camp.

sentences.zh: 陽、鴻等自以深固，不復設備，吏士乘銳，遂大破平之。

translations.literal: Yang, Hong and the like thought themselves deep and firm, no longer set defenses; clerks and soldiers rode keenness; thereupon greatly defeated and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: Bu Yang and Pan Hong thought themselves safe deep in the hills and let their guard drop; Du Shang's men struck while their anger was hot and crushed them.

sentences.zh: 尚出兵三年，群寇悉定。

translations.literal: Shang sent out troops three years; mass bandits were all settled.
translations.idiomatic: Three years of campaigning left the region clear of bandits.

sentences.zh: 七年，封右鄉侯，遷桂陽太守。

translations.literal: In the seventh year, he was enfeoffed as marquis of You township, transferred to grand administrator of Guiyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year he was ennobled as marquis of You district and made grand administrator of Guiyang.

sentences.zh: 明年，徵還京師。

translations.literal: The next year, he was summoned back to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: The following year he was recalled to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 時荊州兵朱蓋等，征戍役久，財賞不贍，忿恚，復作亂，與桂陽賊胡蘭等三千餘人復攻桂陽，焚燒郡縣，太守任胤棄城走，賊眾遂至數萬。

translations.literal: At the time the Jingzhou troops Zhu Gai and others, long in campaign garrison service, funds and rewards not sufficient, resented and hated; again they made chaos, and with three thousand-odd men of Guiyang bandit Hu Lan and others again attacked Guiyang, burned commandery and counties; Grand Administrator Ren Yin abandoned the city and fled; the bandit host thereupon reached several tens of thousands.
translations.idiomatic: Jingzhou soldiers led by Zhu Gai had been on garrison duty for years without pay. They mutinied, joined three thousand men under the Guiyang rebel Hu Lan, stormed the commandery, and burned the towns. Prefect Ren Yin fled, and the horde swelled to tens of thousands.

sentences.zh: 轉攻零陵，太守陳球固守拒之。

translations.literal: They turned to attack Lingling; Grand Administrator Chen Qiu firmly defended and resisted them.
translations.idiomatic: They swung toward Lingling, but Prefect Chen Qiu held the walls.

sentences.zh: 於是以尚為中郎將，將幽、冀、黎陽、烏桓步騎二萬六千人救球，又與長沙太守抗徐等發諸郡兵，并埶討擊，大破之，斬蘭等首三千五百級，餘賊走蒼梧。

translations.literal: Thereupon Shang was appointed General of the Household, leading twenty-six thousand infantry and cavalry of You, Ji, Liyang, and the Wuhuan to rescue Qiu; moreover together with Grand Administrator of Changsha Kang Xu and others issued troops of the various commanderies, joining forces to strike and attack, greatly defeated them, beheaded Lan and others to three thousand five hundred levels; remaining bandits fled to Cangwu.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang was named general of the household at the head of twenty-six thousand foot and horse from Youzhou, Ji province, Liyang, and the Wuhuan tribes to relieve Chen Qiu. With Kang Xu of Changsha he mobilized county levies, smashed the rebels, took thirty-five hundred heads including Hu Lan's, and drove the survivors toward Cangwu.

sentences.zh: 詔賜尚錢百萬，餘人各有差。

translations.literal: An edict granted Shang one million cash; the rest each had gradations.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor awarded Du Shang a million cash and scaled bonuses for the others.

sentences.zh: 時抗徐與尚俱為名將，數有功。

translations.literal: At the time Kang Xu and Shang together were famous generals, repeatedly had merit.
translations.idiomatic: Kang Xu and Du Shang were both celebrated commanders with many victories.

sentences.zh: 徐字伯徐，丹陽人，鄉邦稱其膽智。

translations.literal: Xu, courtesy name Boxu, was a man of Danyang; village and state praised his courage and wisdom.
translations.idiomatic: Kang Xu, styled Boxu, was a native of Danyang whom his countrymen praised for courage and cunning.

sentences.zh: 初試守宣城長，悉移深林遠藪椎髻鳥語之人置於縣下，由是境內無復盜賊。

translations.literal: At first he was probationary guardian magistrate of Xuancheng; he entirely moved the people of deep forests and distant marshes with topknots and bird speech to below the county; from this within the borders there were no more bandits.
translations.idiomatic: His first post was acting magistrate of Xuancheng, where he resettled forest tribes who wore topknots and spoke their own tongues under county administration, after which banditry ceased.

sentences.zh: 後為中郎將宗資別部司馬，擊太山賊公孫舉等，破平之，斬首三千餘級，封烏程東鄉侯五百戶。

translations.literal: Later he was separate division major under General of the Household Zong Zi; he attacked Taishan bandits Gongsun Ju and others, defeated and pacified them, beheaded more than three thousand levels; he was enfeoffed as marquis of Eastern township of Wucheng with five hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: He became a major under General Zong Zi, crushed the Taishan rebels under Gongsun Ju, took three thousand heads, and was ennobled as marquis of Eastern Township in Wucheng with five hundred households.

sentences.zh: 遷太山都尉，寇盜望風奔亡。

translations.literal: He was transferred to commandant of Taishan; bandits and robbers at the wind fled.
translations.idiomatic: As Taishan commandant he scattered every outlaw who heard his name.

sentences.zh: 及在長沙，宿賊皆平。

translations.literal: When he was in Changsha, old bandits were all pacified.
translations.idiomatic: At Changsha he finished off the entrenched bandits.

sentences.zh: 卒於官。

translations.literal: He died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office.

sentences.zh: 桓帝下詔追增封徐五百戶，并前千戶。

translations.literal: Emperor Huan issued an edict posthumously increasing Xu's enfeoffment by five hundred households, together with before one thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan posthumously added five hundred households to his fief, for one thousand in all.

sentences.zh: 復以尚為荊州刺史。

translations.literal: Again Shang was made governor of Jingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang was again named inspector of Jingzhou.

sentences.zh: 尚見胡蘭餘黨南走蒼梧，懼為己負，乃偽上言蒼梧賊入荊州界，於是徵交阯刺史張磐下廷尉。

translations.literal: Shang saw Hu Lan's remaining faction fled south to Cangwu; he feared it would be his burden; thereupon falsely submitted upward saying Cangwu bandits had entered Jingzhou border; thereupon Governor of Jiaozhi Zhang Pan was summoned down to the commandant of justice.
translations.idiomatic: When Hu Lan's remnants slipped south into Cangwu, Du Shang feared blame would fall on him. He falsely reported that Cangwu bandits had crossed into Jingzhou, and Inspector Zhang Pan of Jiaozhi was hauled before the commandant of justice.

sentences.zh: 辭狀未正，會赦見原。

translations.literal: The plea record was not yet corrected; it happened an amnesty saw him forgiven.
translations.idiomatic: His case was still open when a general amnesty cleared him.

sentences.zh: 磐不肯出獄，方更牢持械節，獄吏謂磐曰：「天恩曠然而君不出，何乎？

translations.literal: Pan was unwilling to leave prison; he further firmly grasped fetters and staff; the prison clerk said to Pan: "Heaven's grace is vast yet you do not emerge—why?"
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Pan refused to leave jail, gripping his chains and seal tighter. The warden asked, "The emperor has pardoned you—why stay bound?"

sentences.zh: 」磐因自列曰：「前長沙賊胡蘭作難荊州，餘黨散入交阯。

translations.literal: " Pan therefore submitted in his own line, saying: "Formerly the Changsha bandit Hu Lan raised difficulty in Jingzhou; remaining factions scattered into Jiaozhi."
translations.idiomatic: " Zhang Pan replied: "When Hu Lan of Changsha rebelled in Jingzhou, his followers scattered into Jiaozhi.

sentences.zh: 磐身嬰甲冑，涉危履險，討擊凶患，斬殄渠帥，餘盡鳥竄冒遁，還奔荊州。

translations.literal: Pan personally bore armor and helmet, crossed peril and trod danger, attacked and struck fierce harm, beheaded and exterminated chieftains; the rest all fled like birds rushing escape, returned fleeing to Jingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: I wore armor, took every risk, killed their leaders, and drove the rest like birds back toward Jingzhou.

sentences.zh: 刺史度尚懼磐先言，怖畏罪戾，伏奏見誣。

translations.literal: Governor Du Shang feared Pan would speak first, feared crime and guilt, submitted prostrate that he was slandered.
translations.idiomatic: Inspector Du Shang, afraid I would speak first, panicked and memorialized that I had slandered him.

sentences.zh: 磐備位方伯，為國爪牙，而為尚所枉，受罪牢獄。

translations.literal: Pan occupies the place of a regional lord, is the state's claws and fangs, yet was wronged by Shang, suffered punishment in prison.
translations.idiomatic: I hold a provincial post and serve as the emperor's arm, yet Du Shang's false charge landed me in chains.

sentences.zh: 夫事有虛實，法有是非。

translations.literal: Matters have vacuity and substance; the law has wrong and right.
translations.idiomatic: Facts can be true or false; the law distinguishes right from wrong.

sentences.zh: 磐實不辜，赦無所除。

translations.literal: Pan in substance is not guilty; the amnesty has nothing to remove.
translations.idiomatic: I am innocent; an amnesty cannot erase a crime I never committed.

sentences.zh: 如忍以苟免，永受侵辱之恥，生為惡吏，死為敝鬼。

translations.literal: If I endure to seek bare escape, forever suffer the shame of encroaching insult; alive be a wicked clerk, dead be a worn ghost.
translations.idiomatic: If I accept release now, I live forever disgraced—a corrupt official in life, a shamed ghost in death.

sentences.zh: 乞傳尚詣廷尉，面對曲直，足明真偽。

translations.literal: I beg to summon Shang to the commandant of justice, face to face crooked and straight, sufficient to clarify true and false.
translations.idiomatic: Summon Du Shang to the commandant's court so we can confront each other and expose the truth.

sentences.zh: 尚不徵者，磐埋骨牢檻，終不虛出，望塵受枉。

translations.literal: If Shang is not summoned, Pan buries bones in prison bars, will not vainly emerge, look at dust and suffer wrong.
translations.idiomatic: If Du Shang is not called to testify, I will die in this cell rather than walk free under a false verdict.

sentences.zh: 」廷尉以其狀上，詔書徵尚到廷尉，辭窮受罪，以先有功得原。

translations.literal: " The commandant of justice submitted his petition upward; an edict summoned Shang to the commandant of justice; words exhausted, he suffered punishment; because he earlier had merit he obtained forgiveness.
translations.idiomatic: " The commandant relayed the petition; Du Shang was summoned, broke under questioning, and was punished but pardoned because of past service.

sentences.zh: 磐字子石，丹陽人，以清白稱，終於廬江太守。

translations.literal: Pan, courtesy name Zishi, was a man of Danyang, praised for purity and whiteness; he ended as grand administrator of Lujiang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Pan, styled Zishi, was a Danyang native famed for integrity and ended his career as grand administrator of Lujiang.

sentences.zh: 尚後為遼東太守，數月，鮮卑率兵攻尚，與戰，破之，戎狄憚畏。

translations.literal: Later Shang became grand administrator of Liaodong; after several months, the Xianbei led troops to attack Shang; he fought them and defeated them; Rong and Di feared and dreaded.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang later governed Liaodong; within months the Xianbei attacked, and he defeated them—the tribes learned to fear him.

sentences.zh: 年五十，延熹九年，卒於官。

translations.literal: Age fifty; in the ninth year of Yanxi, he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in Yanxi 9 (166 CE) at the age of fifty.

sentences.zh: 楊琁字機平，會稽烏傷人也。

translations.literal: Yang Xuan, courtesy name Jiping, was a man of Wushang in Kuaiji.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xuan, styled Jiping, came from Wushang in Kuaiji commandery.

sentences.zh: 高祖父茂，本河東人，從光武征伐，為威寇將軍，封烏傷新陽鄉侯。

translations.literal: His great-grandfather Mao was originally a man of Hedong; he followed Guangwu in campaigns, became General Who Might Smite the Bandits, was enfeoffed as marquis of Xinyang township of Wushang.
translations.idiomatic: His great-grandfather Yang Mao was a Hedong man who campaigned with Emperor Guangwu as General Who Subdues Raiders and received the marquisate of Xinyang township in Wushang.

sentences.zh: 建武中就國，傳封三世，有罪國除，因而家焉。

translations.literal: In Jianwu he went to his state; the enfeoffment passed three generations; having crime the state was abolished; therefore they made home there.
translations.idiomatic: In the Jianwu era he took up his fief; the title passed three generations until a crime forfeited it, and the family remained there.

sentences.zh: 父扶，交阯刺史，有理能名。

translations.literal: His father Fu was governor of Jiaozhi, had a name for governing ability.
translations.idiomatic: His father Yang Fu served as inspector of Jiaozhi and was known as a capable administrator.

sentences.zh: 兄喬，為尚書，容儀偉麗，數上言政事，桓帝愛其才貌，詔妻以公主，喬固辭不聽，遂閉口不食，七日而死。

translations.literal: His elder brother Qiao was secretariat director; countenance and bearing were great and beautiful; repeatedly submitted speech on government affairs; Emperor Huan loved his talent and appearance; edict married him to a princess; Qiao firmly declined and was not listened to; thereupon closed mouth and did not eat, died in seven days.
translations.idiomatic: His elder brother Yang Qiao was a secretary; he was handsome and often advised the throne. Emperor Huan admired his looks and talent and offered him an imperial princess. Qiao refused again and again; when the emperor would not relent, he stopped eating and died on the seventh day.

sentences.zh: 琁初舉孝廉，稍遷，靈帝時為零陵太守。

translations.literal: At first Xuan was recommended as Filially Pious and Incorrupt; he was gradually promoted; in the time of Emperor Ling he became grand administrator of Lingling.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as filially pious and incorrupt, Yang Xuan rose through several posts until Emperor Ling appointed him grand administrator of Lingling.

sentences.zh: 是時蒼梧、桂陽猾賊相聚，攻郡縣，賊眾多而琁力弱，吏人憂恐。

translations.literal: At this time the cunning bandits of Cangwu and Guiyang gathered together, attacked commanderies and counties; the bandits were many but Xuan's strength was weak; clerks and people worried and feared.
translations.idiomatic: Cunning rebels from Cangwu and Guiyang had joined forces and were storming county seats. Yang Xuan was badly outnumbered, and his officials despaired.

sentences.zh: 琁乃特制馬車數十乘，以排囊盛石灰於車上，繫布索於馬尾，又為兵車，專彀弓弩，剋共會戰。

translations.literal: Xuan then specially made several tens of horse-drawn carts, using bellows-bags to hold lime on the carts, tied cloth ropes to horses' tails; moreover he made war carts, exclusively drawing crossbows, scheduled a joint battle.
translations.idiomatic: He had dozens of wagons built, loaded leather bellows with lime on the decks, tied hemp ropes to the horses' tails, and paired them with combat carts fitted with crossbows, then set a day for the decisive engagement.

sentences.zh: 乃令馬車居前，順風鼓灰，賊不得視，因以火燒布，然馬驚，奔突賊陣，因使後車弓弩亂發，鉦鼓鳴震。

translations.literal: Thereupon he ordered horse carts to go in front, along the wind drumming dust; the bandits could not see; thereupon with fire burned the cloth; then horses were startled, rushed and burst the bandit formation; thereupon ordered rear carts' crossbows to fire wildly; gongs and drums sounded and shook.
translations.idiomatic: The lime wagons advanced upwind, blinding the enemy with dust; his men set the tail-ropes alight, panicking the horses into the rebel ranks while the archery carts behind cut loose and drums and bronze clappers thundered.

sentences.zh: 群盜波駭破散，追逐傷斬無數，梟其渠帥，郡境以清。

translations.literal: The mass bandits were wave-frightened, broken and scattered; pursuit wounded and beheaded innumerable; they displayed the heads of the chieftains; the commandery territory was thereby cleared.
translations.idiomatic: The mob broke like surf; the pursuit left countless dead and wounded and the ringleaders' heads on stakes. Order returned to the commandery.

sentences.zh: 荊州刺史趙凱，誣奏琁實非身破賊，而妄有其功。

translations.literal: Governor of Jingzhou Zhao Kai falsely memorialized that Xuan in truth had not personally destroyed the bandits but falsely possessed merit.
translations.idiomatic: Inspector Zhao Kai of Jingzhou memorialized that Yang Xuan had never personally defeated the rebels and had falsely claimed the credit.

sentences.zh: 琁與相章奏，凱有黨助，遂檻車徵琁。

translations.literal: Xuan exchanged memorials with him; Kai had factional helpers; thereupon a cage-cart summoned Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xuan answered with his own memorials, but Zhao Kai had allies at court and had Yang summoned back to the capital in a prison cart.

sentences.zh: 防禁嚴密，無由自訟，乃噬臂出血，書衣為章，具陳破賊形埶，及言凱所誣狀，潛令親屬詣闕通之。

translations.literal: Defense and prohibition were strict and dense; there was no means to sue himself; therefore he bit his arm and drew blood, wrote on clothing as a memorial, fully stated the situation of destroying bandits, and spoke of Kai's false accusation state; secretly ordered relatives to go to the palace gate and submit it.
translations.idiomatic: Under tight guard he could not plead his case, so he tore his arm until it bled and wrote a petition in blood on his robe, detailing how he had crushed the rebels and how Zhao Kai had slandered him, then smuggled it to the palace through kinsmen.

sentences.zh: 詔書原琁，拜議郎，凱反受誣人之罪。

translations.literal: An edict forgave Xuan; he was appointed gentleman consultant; Kai instead suffered the crime of the false accuser.
translations.idiomatic: An edict cleared Yang Xuan and named him gentleman consultant; Zhao Kai was convicted instead as the false accuser.

sentences.zh: 琁三遷為勃海太守，所在有異政，以事免。

translations.literal: Xuan after three promotions became grand administrator of Bohai; wherever he went he had unusual governance; he was dismissed because of an affair.
translations.idiomatic: After three further promotions he governed Bohai with a notable record, then lost office over an unrelated matter.

sentences.zh: 後尚書令張溫特表薦之，徵拜尚書僕射。

translations.literal: Later Secretariat Director Zhang Wen specially memorial-recommended him; he was summoned and appointed deputy director of the secretariat.
translations.idiomatic: Secretariat Director Zhang Wen singled him out for recommendation, and the court summoned him as deputy director of the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 以病乞骸骨，卒於家。

translations.literal: Because of illness he begged for his old bones; he died at home.
translations.idiomatic: He retired citing illness and died at home.

sentences.zh: 論曰：安順以後，風威稍薄，寇攘寖橫，緣隙而生，剽人盜邑者不闋時月，假署皇王者蓋以十數。

translations.literal: The discussion says: After An and Shun, wind and awe gradually thinned; bandit raid gradually ran rampant; born along cracks; those who plunder people and steal towns do not cease for months; those who falsely enthrone as emperor-king perhaps number by tens.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: After the reigns of Emperors An and Shun, imperial prestige waned and rebellion spread wherever there was an opening. Month after month raiders seized towns and proclaimed themselves kings—such pretenders must have numbered in the dozens.

sentences.zh: 或託驗神道，或矯妄冕服。

translations.literal: Some relied on verifying the divine way; some falsely wore imperial caps and robes.
translations.idiomatic: Some claimed supernatural sanction; others dressed in counterfeit imperial regalia.

sentences.zh: 然其雄渠魁長，未有聞焉，猶至壘盈四郊，奔命首尾。

translations.literal: Yet their fierce chieftains and chief leaders were not yet heard of; still they reached ramparts filling the four suburbs, rushing orders head to tail.
translations.idiomatic: Few of these warlords earned lasting fame, yet their camps ringed every suburb and kept couriers galloping without rest.

sentences.zh: 若夫數將者，並宣力勤慮，以勞定功，而景風之賞未甄，膚受之言互及。

translations.literal: As for the several generals, together they proclaimed strength and diligent thought; by labor they fixed merit; yet timely breeze rewards were not distinguished; skin-received words mutually reached.
translations.idiomatic: The commanders named here strained every nerve and earned peace through hardship, yet timely rewards from the throne went undistributed while spiteful gossip from court struck them in turn.

sentences.zh: 以此而推，政道難乎以免。

translations.literal: By this to infer, the way of government is difficult thereby to avoid.
translations.idiomatic: Small wonder sound government proved almost impossible.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：張宗裨禹，敢殿後拒。

translations.literal: The praise says: Zhang Zong aided Yu, dared rear guard resistance.
translations.idiomatic: The ode runs: Zhang Zong served Deng Yu and dared hold the rear guard alone.

sentences.zh: 江、淮、海、岱，虔劉寇阻。

translations.literal: Yangzi, Huai, sea, Dai—slaughtered and ravaged, bandits blocked.
translations.idiomatic: From the Yangzi and Huai to the coast and Mount Tai, slaughter and rebel strongholds blocked the land.

sentences.zh: 其誰清之？

translations.literal: Who cleared them?
translations.idiomatic: Who swept them clean?

sentences.zh: 雄、尚、緄、撫。

translations.literal: Xiong, Shang, Gun, Fu.
translations.idiomatic: Fa Xiong, Du Shang, Feng Gun, and Teng Fu.

sentences.zh: 琁能用譎，亦云振旅。

translations.literal: Xuan was able to use stratagem; also called shaking the troops.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xuan won with guile and marched his army home in good order.
